name: CSS transitions & animations
strengths: GPU-composited transform/opacity, no JS, no hydration cost, cannot regress TBT
best_for: hover/focus/active states, simple enter-exit, micro-interactions, anything a single property change can express
avoid_when: orchestrated multi-step sequences, gesture-driven motion, animating between layout positions
reduced_motion: wrap every rule in @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) and reset to no-op
ssr_notes: zero cost; works with SSR and no-JS

name: View Transitions API
weight_note: 0 KB - native browser API; verify current browser support
strengths: native cross-state and cross-document morphs, browser handles the tween
best_for: page/route transitions, list-to-detail morphs, MPA navigation
avoid_when: fine-grained control, older browsers without a fallback path
reduced_motion: honour prefers-reduced-motion manually - the API does not opt out for you
ssr_notes: works with MPA and SSR; progressive enhancement

name: Motion (formerly Framer Motion)
weight_note: verify current size against the release you install; vendor claims APIs up to 90% smaller than the GSAP equivalent - treat as their marketing claim, not measured fact
strengths: declarative API, layout animations, AnimatePresence exit transitions, gestures/drag, springs, hybrid JS + hardware-accelerated engine
best_for: React/Vue application UI, list and route transitions, gesture-driven interactions, shared-layout morphs
avoid_when: a plain CSS transition would do, or the work is a long scroll-timeline narrative (GSAP suits that better)
reduced_motion: useReducedMotion() hook, or MotionConfig reducedMotion='user' at the root
ssr_notes: client-side component - mark 'use client' in Next app router
license: MIT core; Motion+ extras are paid

name: GSAP + plugins
weight_note: core plus each plugin adds up - verify the built size with only the plugins you import
strengths: precise timelines, ScrollTrigger scroll sequencing, SplitText, Flip, MorphSVG/DrawSVG, framework-agnostic
best_for: scroll-driven narrative sequences, complex orchestrated timelines, SVG morphing, marketing spectacle
avoid_when: simple React UI state transitions where Motion is more idiomatic, or when CSS suffices
reduced_motion: gsap.matchMedia() with a (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) branch that sets no-op tweens
ssr_notes: needs a useLayoutEffect/useGSAP guard so tweens do not run during SSR
license: licensing changed after the Webflow acquisition - VERIFY current terms for core and each plugin

name: AutoAnimate
weight_note: very small - one utility, verify against current release
strengths: one line to animate add/remove/reorder of children, near-zero API surface
best_for: lists, tables, accordions, any container whose children change
avoid_when: bespoke or choreographed motion - it deliberately gives you one behaviour
reduced_motion: respects prefers-reduced-motion automatically - verify on the version you install
ssr_notes: attach via ref in an effect; no SSR output

name: react-spring
strengths: physics-based springs, imperative and declarative control, shares an API with React Native
best_for: natural spring motion, drag/physics interactions, teams already on React Native
avoid_when: the team wants the simplest declarative path (Motion) or CSS is enough
reduced_motion: gate with useReducedMotion and swap to immediate: true

name: Lottie
weight_note: player plus the animation JSON payload - the asset often outweighs the player; verify both
strengths: plays designer-authored After Effects animation exactly as designed
best_for: brand/illustrative set pieces handed over by a motion designer
avoid_when: perf-sensitive pages, simple motion, or anything CSS/Motion can express
reduced_motion: pause and show a static frame under prefers-reduced-motion - the player will not do it for you
ssr_notes: render client-side only; lazy-load the player and the asset
license: MIT player; asset licence is your own

name: Three.js / React Three Fiber
weight_note: the heaviest option by a wide margin - always code-split it; verify the built chunk
strengths: full WebGL/3D, particle systems, shaders, signature hero spectacle
best_for: a genuine 3D or particle hero moment that the brief actually calls for
avoid_when: marketing pages under a performance budget, low-power devices, anything where a gradient would do
reduced_motion: must ship a static fallback - do not merely slow the scene down
ssr_notes: never SSR the canvas; dynamic-import with ssr disabled and lazy-mount below the fold
